title: "Trump Can't End This War — If He Loses Power, He Goes to Prison | Prof Jiang"
full_text: So, a lot of people are asking me right now, why is Trump doubling down in Iran? Why is he sending more troops? Why isn't he just declaring victory and coming home? And I think most people are missing what's actually happening here. This isn't about Iran. This isn't about oil. This isn't even about China. This is about Trump's survival. And I want to walk you through why I believe Trump cannot end this war. because if he loses power, he goes to prison. Now, before we get into this, I need you to understand something about how empires behave when they're in decline. And this is really important because if you don't understand this pattern, you won't understand what's happening right now in the Middle East. When empires are failing, they suffer from a very specific and lethal combination of both hubris and desperation. They become so arrogant that they refuse to consider the possibility of defeat. And at the same time, they're so desperate for a knockout punch that they continue to escalate. And this is exactly what we're seeing with Trump right now. Let me explain the game theory here because this is crucial. Trump cannot afford to leave office. And we know this because we know exactly what happened in his first term. Remember the raid on Mara Lago? Remember all the lawfare against Trump? Remember the court cases that almost bankrupted him. So you can imagine that there are a lot of people who want revenge for what Trump is doing in his second term. The Democrats have made it very clear that if they come back to power, they're going after him and his family. This isn't speculation. We've seen this play out already. So Trump's personal calculus right now is very simple. How do I stay in power? and staying in power through the ballot box is extremely risky. But if he starts a war, and this war is at first supported by both Democrats and Republicans, but then slowly this war spirals out of control, and then there's unrest in America, in which case he's forced to send in the National Guard, and then he's forced to call a national draft in order to protect American interests in the Middle East. then he can conceivably stay in power for as long as this war continues. And we know this is possible because of what happened with Zalinski in Ukraine. Remember, because of the war in Ukraine, they suspended their constitution. They suspended elections and Zalinski is still in power. In fact, when Zalinski visited Trump in the White House, Trump asked him, "Hey, you can really do that." and Zalinsky said, "You like this, don't you?" So, I think unfortunately that is going through the mind of Trump right now. But let me step back because a lot of people are saying, "Can't Trump just declare victory and go home?" And the problem with this is if Trump were to leave the Middle East, basically the American empire would collapse. So, let me explain to you what would happen if Trump just said, "You know what? I killed the leadership. I destroyed the nuclear weapons program. I set back the ballistics program. Everything I wanted to accomplish, I've already accomplished. I'm going to go home. Goodbye. Here's what happens. Iran would say to the GCC, "Hey, you destroyed our country. You have to pay back reparations. If you don't pay back reparations, first of all, we'll continue to attack you and destroy your oil infrastructure. And second of all, we won't let you use the Strait of Hormuz." And so now the GCC is forced to take $4 trillion in sovereign wealth funds. Before this money was going to the United States. Now it's going to Iran to rebuild their economy and infrastructure and their military. But then the Japanese and the South Koreans are looking at this and they're thinking, "Well, we just saw what happened. The Americans attacked Iran. Iran fought back and destroyed the GCC. And the Americans refused to defend the GCC." Remember the entire point of the petro dollar agreement is that Americans would guarantee the security of the GCC. And when this war started, what happened? The Americans abandoned their bases, changed into civilian clothes, and moved into hotels because they could not stop the Iranians from reigning drones and ballistic missiles on their bases. So, the South Koreans and the Japanese will say, "Okay, well, we can't trust the Americans, so we need to remilitarize as soon as possible. let's just not depend on the Americans and remilitarize. But what that also means is they stop buying US treasuries. And so the US dollar loses buyers from the Middle East and loses buyers from East Asia. And the dollar could potentially collapse in value. And now America has $40 trillion in debt, which forces an economic crisis in America. A great reset, financial depression. You'll have riots in the streets. And then you go to Europe. The Europeans are saying the Americans are suffering from depression and the Americans are not able to defend their allies. Why are we fighting the Russians again? Why don't we just come to an agreement with the Russians? Why are we sending our men to die in Ukraine? So now Russia becomes the dominant power in Europe and Iran becomes the dominant power in the Middle East and America is imploding back in the Western Hemisphere. Is that a situation that the American government that Trump would accept? I don't think it is. So, there's really no possibility of America retreating from this war. America can only escalate, unfortunately. Now, let me talk about what's actually happening on the ground right now because this is where it gets really interesting. Right now, a Marine Expeditionary Force is traveling from Japan to the Middle East. We should expect them to arrive in about a week. There's about 2,000 of them and they specialize in amphibious landing. So, the idea is that they will bombard the coastline of Car Island and this will enable the Marines to land and secure the oil deposits of Car Island. And at this point, Trump will declare victory and try to get the Iranians to the negotiating table and say, "Listen, I have Car Island and this is your economic lifeline. So, let's cut a deal that allows us both to save face." And I think that is the plan. But there are a lot of issues with this plan. The first issue is that Carg Island is not as important economically as people believe. Even if you secure Car Island, Iran has workarounds where they can just ship from the coast. Issue number two is that Iran needs financing for this war and they're using oil as collateral financing. But if the Chinese and the Russians believe that the Americans want to strangle Iran, it's not in their best interest to see Iran destroyed. So, they're going to provide financing anyway. And look, the reality is that now that oil is shooting up in price, this benefits Russia tremendously. Putin can take all these oil riches and finance Iran in fighting this war because Russia has the most to lose if Iran loses this war and there's regime change. If Iran were to fall, this opens up the Russian southern flank to the Americans because Azerbaijan is an Israeli American ally. And if Iran were to fall, then the Americans and Israelis could stage operations from the south. But here's the most important problem. Once you take Car Island, you expose the Marines to drone strikes and artillery strikes from the coast, which means that you now need to send in more Marines to secure the coast. So you send in 2,000 3,000 troops to secure Car Island. Done that easily. But they can't secure the island against Iranian drones. So the only way you can do that is by taking the coastline along Car Island. You do that with tens of thousands of Marines. But then you expose these Marines to attacks from the mountains, the Zagros mountains, these drones, these missiles from the Zagros Mountains. Which means that now you have to invest 100,000 troops to go after Iran's underground missile facilities. And this is exactly what happened in Vietnam. The great irony of all this is that the Marine Expeditionary Force that is going to Iran is the same force that went into Vietnam and marked the first major ground involvement of the Americans in about 1965. They went into Daang and secured the Air Force base. And at first it was like 3,500 soldiers and that was supposed to be it. like five years later, you're at 500,000 American soldiers. So once you send in ground troops, it creates a logic, a momentum of its own. And then you have mission creep and then sunk cost fallacy. And then eventually you have a national draft. And that is the great danger of taking Car Island at this point. Now, I want to be very clear about what I think is going to happen because I think Trump is going to take Car Island. he'll do it very easily and then he'll want to secure the coast as well. And so you have mission creep. And unfortunately he's being egged on by both Israel and the GCC because Israel clearly wants this war to escalate. The GCC wants to remove the Iranian threat as soon as possible because they see the amount of damage that the Iranians can do to the GCC. So Dubai, if nothing changes, Dubai will just cease to exist because Dubai is just based on the perception that it's a safe tax haven. But if Iran can just re drones on Dubai uncontested, then no one's going to put their money in Dubai. So for Dubai to continue to thrive, it needs to destroy Iran and remove that threat from the Middle East. So unfortunately the way that things are structured suggests that this war can only escalate and not actually move towards a ceasefire. And Trump because of his personal situation, because of the threat of prison, because of what the Democrats have promised to do to him if he leaves office, he cannot afford to back down. He cannot afford to admit defeat. He can only double down. And this is the pattern we see with empires in decline. They become so arrogant and so insular that they refuse to consider the possibility of defeat. So they always double down. And I don't know how you resolve this issue. It isn't going to be easy. That is for sure. So when people ask me how does this end, I have to tell them I don't think it ends well. I think we're looking at a protracted conflict. I think we're looking at mission creep. I think we're looking at a national draft. And I think we're looking at Trump using this war to stay in power because the alternative for him is prison. And that's not a choice. That's an existential threat. And people facing existential threats don't make rational decisions. They make survival decisions.
